incident: Panic Buying Empties Shelves Across Region
description: [UPDATE 0:54] Yellowstone implemented emergency purchase limits at stores ($200K custom) and launched food desert outreach with supply bags for rural households ($200K custom). Redwood (EMA) coordinated regional resupply and emergency food distribution ($100K custom) and sign repair coordination with VDOT. Jasper issued a public grid status transparency update ($0 custom) reducing blackout fears. Yoho deployed crews to fix I-81 signs ($150K custom). Purchase limits slowing hoarding. Fuel shortages continue affecting some emergency vehicle operations. The worst panic has been contained but supply chain normalization is ongoing. Reduced consequences will carry into Phase 3.
